channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-1312] crash at boot time in isci driver with 2.6.18-308.1.1
item.description:
Jeff Johnson reported on lustre-discuss that he can't boot with our patched RHEL5 & RHEL6 kernel, while the stock kernel works just fine. The stack trace is the following:

Starting system logger: ----------- [cut here ] --------- [please
bite here ] ---------
Kernel BUG at drivers/scsi/isci/request.h:348
invalid opcode: 0000 [1] SMP
last sysfs file: /class/net/eth0/address
CPU 0

uhci_hcd(U) ohci_hcd(U) ehci_hcd(U)
Pid: 1337, comm: kjournald Tainted: G     ---- 2.6.18.lustre18 #1
RIP: 0010:[<ffffffff88117b22>]  [<ffffffff88117b22>]
:isci:sci_request_build_sgl+0x110/0x1e4

R10: ffff81023ea0e100 R11: 0000000000000060 R12: 0000000000000003

FS:  0000000000000000(0000) GS:ffffffff80431000(0000)
knlGS:0000000000000000

CR2: 00002b81aeba0380 CR3: 000000043b96a000 CR4: 00000000000006a0
Process kjournald (pid: 1337, threadinfo ffff81023f8e0000, task
ffff81043fdcc7a0)
Stack:  0000000000000001 ffff81023eaf7a00 000000000000004d
ffff81023eaf7000
 ffff81023ea0e100 000000000009f000 0000000000000001 ffff81023eaf7801
 ffff8102472841f8 ffffffff88117c28 ffff81043f12f400 0000000000000004
Call Trace:
 [<ffffffff88117c28>]
:isci:sci_stp_optimized_request_construct+0x32/0x6c
 [<ffffffff88119a04>] :isci:isci_request_execute+0x5b6/0x82e
 [<ffffffff88120d20>] :isci:isci_task_execute_task+0x10e/0x23e
 [<ffffffff88106f6f>] :libsas:sas_ata_qc_issue+0x1fb/0x286
 [<ffffffff880776b7>] :scsi_mod:scsi_done+0x0/0x18
 [<ffffffff880ceb55>] :libata:ata_qc_issue+0x4ef/0x567
 [<ffffffff880d2ef0>] :libata:ata_scsi_rw_xlat+0x119/0x188
 [<ffffffff880776b7>] :scsi_mod:scsi_done+0x0/0x18
 [<ffffffff880d2dd7>] :libata:ata_scsi_rw_xlat+0x0/0x188
 [<ffffffff880d309f>] :libata:ata_scsi_translate+0x140/0x16d
 [<ffffffff880776b7>] :scsi_mod:scsi_done+0x0/0x18
 [<ffffffff88106119>] :libsas:sas_queuecommand+0x86/0x2a3
 [<ffffffff8001cd72>] __mod_timer+0xff/0x10e
 [<ffffffff800155a2>] sync_buffer+0x0/0x3f
 [<ffffffff800155a2>] sync_buffer+0x0/0x3f
 [<ffffffff88077d9d>] :scsi_mod:scsi_dispatch_cmd+0x297/0x351
 [<ffffffff8807d55a>] :scsi_mod:scsi_request_fn+0x2c3/0x392
 [<ffffffff8005a43f>] generic_unplug_device+0x22/0x32
 [<ffffffff800155d8>] sync_buffer+0x36/0x3f
 [<ffffffff80063a0a>] __wait_on_bit+0x40/0x6e
 [<ffffffff800155a2>] sync_buffer+0x0/0x3f
 [<ffffffff80063aa4>] out_of_line_wait_on_bit+0x6c/0x78
 [<ffffffff800a34ca>] wake_bit_function+0x0/0x23
 [<ffffffff880342fc>] :jbd:journal_commit_transaction+0xa7f/0x132b
 [<ffffffff8003d80e>] lock_timer_base+0x1b/0x3c
 [<ffffffff88038469>] :jbd:kjournald+0xc1/0x213
 [<ffffffff800a349c>] autoremove_wake_function+0x0/0x2e
 [<ffffffff880383a8>] :jbd:kjournald+0x0/0x213
 [<ffffffff800a3284>] keventd_create_kthread+0x0/0xc4
 [<ffffffff80032679>] kthread+0xfe/0x132
 [<ffffffff8005dfb1>] child_rip+0xa/0x11
 [<ffffffff800a3284>] keventd_create_kthread+0x0/0xc4
 [<ffffffff8003257b>] kthread+0x0/0x132
 [<ffffffff8005dfa7>] child_rip+0x0/0x11


Code: 0f 0b 68 6b 4b 12 88 c2 5c 01 eb fe 48 89 c2 48 03 55 50 48
RIP  [<ffffffff88117b22>] :isci:sci_request_build_sgl+0x110/0x1e4
 RSP<ffff81023f8e19e0>
<0>Kernel panic - not syncing: Fatal exception

The isci driver manages the new SAS onboard interface embedded in the Intel Sandy Bridge-EP (Xeon E5-2600) chipset. According to Jeff, he can reproduce the same issue with the 2.6.32-220.4.2.el6_lustre shipped with lustre 2.2, while the stock kernel from Red Hat works again just fine.

Looking at the stack trace, we hit the following assertion:

static inline dma_addr_t
sci_io_request_get_dma_addr(struct isci_request *ireq, void *virt_addr)
{
        char *requested_addr = (char *)virt_addr;
        char *base_addr = (char *)ireq;

        BUG_ON(requested_addr < base_addr);
        BUG_ON((requested_addr - base_addr) >= sizeof(*ireq)) -> BOOM!!!

The code path is sci_request_build_sgl->to_sgl_element_pair_dma->sci_io_request_get_dma_addr.

I suspect that blkdev_tunables-2.6-rhel5.patch is the culprit. Unfortunately, Jeff cannot retest w/o this patch soon.
item.summary: crash at boot time in isci driver with 2.6.18-308.1.1

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

That's good news! Let's land the patch now!
Thanks for testing the patch.

After test via remote access. I conclude that we have to set isci_sht.sg_tablesize = 128 in rhel5. Since we change MAX_PHYS_SEGMENTS,MAX_HW_SEGMENTS,BLK_DEF_MAX_SECTORS. The sci_io_request_get_dma_addr() will receive a sg_index over its upper limit SCI_MAX_SCATTER_GATHER_ELEMENTS. If restore either MAX_PHYS_SEGMENTS or BLK_DEF_MAX_SECTORS to default value. Then the driver works well. But we will lost performance if restore them. So just set isci driver it still can work in default mode then.

Compiled and successfully booted 2.6.32.lustre22_9c8f747 on the CentOS 6.2 system with isci SAS/SATA driver.

Winner winner, chicken dinner!

Starting looping fio testing now to stress the boot disk and isci interface. I will report back on fio/stress results.

Okay, I see we have a build from the last patch, I am uploading now.
cliffw

–
cliffw
Support Guy
WhamCloud, Inc.
www.whamcloud.com
I have provided Yang Sheng ssh access to the CentOS 5.8/2.6.18-308.1.1 system. Yang, you can try this patch directly or I can. Let me know
master patch commit to: http://review.whamcloud.com/2596
patch upload to: http://review.whamcloud.com/2595
Johann, looks like this really may cause some problem. I'll produce a patch for test.

The isci driver seems to have its own limit for the SG, SCI_MAX_SCATTER_GATHER_ELEMENTS.
I think we should just try to set isci_sht::.sg_tablesize to the default value (i.e. 0xff) instead of the the new one.
Jeff, Yangsheng, any chance to give this a try?

Aggressive testing of system boot disks using fio have been run successfully on 1.8 and 2.2 branch kernels (2.6.18-308.1.1 and 2.6.32-220.4.2) with the blkdev_tunables patch removed.

It appears that the current blkdev_tunables patch negatively impacts stability of the isci SAS/SATA controller kernel driver.

I removed the blkdev_tunables-2.6-rhel6.patch and recompiled. The resulting kernel RPM does boot properly. I am running disk stress tests on the boot disk during the day today to make sure it is stable. So far it appears removing the blkdev_tunables-2.6-rhel6.patch resolves the issue.

My guess is that removing the same patch and building a 1.8 kernel on kernel-2.6.18-308.1.1 and booting it on this Sandy Bridge Xeon platform with the isci onboard SAS/SATA controller would have a similar successful outcome.

Jeff

To be clear, did the most recent results you posted include the blkdev_tunables-2.6-rhel5.patch? As part of isolating the problem we would like to know if removing that patch from the equation alters the behaviour...

Thanks

Peter

This issue also occurs on 1.87-wc1 installs using CentOS 5.8 as well. The issue is the same, standard release CentOS kernels are stable, Whamcloud patched kernels crash. So this impacts 1.8 and 2.2 release trees.

Based on some of the previous crashes I saw I modified the boot disk configuration; from the standard RHEL/CentOS LVM2 based boot partition/FS scheme using ext4 to a more simple three standard partition [boot/root/swap] format with ext2 for boot and ext3 for root. Removing the LVM2/ext4 from the boot drive configuration did not clear the issue.

I also tried several types of boot disk hardware (Seagate, Hitachi, WD, Intel SSD, etc) with no positive change. It appears that this issue is caused by the kernel mods made to support Lustre and those changes impact the Intel isci SAS/SATA driver or one of its dependencies.

I can provide remote access to this platform via ssh if it is useful.

The following are two kernel boot sequences from the same system. kernel-2.6.32-220.4.2.el6_lustre.x86_64 (fails) and kernel-2.6.32-220.4.2.el6.x86_64 (no issue). The hardware is Intel S2600JFQ motherboard, 2x Xeon E5-2660 processors, 32GB ram and a SATA boot drive. The SAS/SATA motherboard interface is the Intel RSTe onboard controller which uses the isci kernel driver.

kernel-2.6.32-220.4.2.el6_lustre.x86_64 (fails)

Linux version 2.6.32-220.4.2.el6_lustre.x86_64 (jenkins@client-31.lab.whamcloud.com) (gcc version 4.4.6 20110731 (Red Hat 4.4.6-3) (GCC) ) #1 SMP Wed Mar 14 13:03:47 PDT 2012
